match.userMessage:
9-panel cinematic storyboard sheet derived from Image 1. Mix Wide, Medium, and Tight shots of the same scene. No borders between panels.

Layout: 3x3 grid (9 panels) in a 16:9 landscape image.

Scene: Modern social-first product photography with tactile editorial energy frames SCARLETT in light casual beach clothing suitable for walking while carrying a canvas tote bag as she bends forward at the waist with arm extended and muscles tensed mid-reach to retrieve the metallic tube from (CORAL_LIPSTICK) lying on wet sand at the Bondi shoreline, water streaming off the metal as the wave recedes leaving delicate foam trails across beige sand and shallow turquoise wash, dynamic ocean edge with gentle rolling waves and white foam in the midground, bright natural daylight creating crisp directional shadows sparkling reflections on water and glossy metal surfaces, high-key airy atmosphere with warm highlights and immediate real light, shallow depth of field keeping the action and product interaction tack sharp against softly blurred sea and horizon, eye-level handheld camera with slight downward angle for immersive natural framing, clean bright color grading with true-to-life tones and one accent color popping against neutral sand and water.

Match Image 1 exactly: same character(s) (face, hair, skin, clothing), lighting, color grade, and texture. If character reference sheets are provided, use them for likeness — do not render them as separate panels.

No text, dialogue bubbles, scene numbers, or watermarks.


  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [PRIMARY SOURCE]: Primary source scene — generate 9 variant shots from this image
    - Image 2 [CHARACTER REF]: Scarlett - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 3 [LOCATION REF]: EXT. BONDI BEACH SHORELINE - DAY - Wet sand shoreline at Bondi Beach where waves gently wash over bare feet
    - Image 4 [ELEMENT REF]: CORAL_LIPSTICK - A slim metallic lipstick tube containing a vivid bright coral bullet
    
    IMPORTANT: Character, location, and element reference images are for IDENTITY CONSISTENCY ONLY. Do NOT reproduce them as separate panels or subjects. Elements (logos, products) must render faithfully when referenced but should appear naturally within the scene. All output panels must depict the scene from the PRIMARY SOURCE image.
  </reference-images>
